Title: MYB61 is regulated by GRF4 and promotes nitrogen utilization and biomass production in rice

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB transcription factor; N-terminal DNA-binding domain and C-terminal transactivation domain
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Nipponbare; Zhonghua11; Dongnong428; Longdao5; NILs with 9311 introgression
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major structural biomass component of the cell wall supporting plant growth, organ architecture, and grain yield; links carbon partitioning to biomass production
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Internodes/cell wall of sclerenchyma fiber cells
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: cell wall cellulose biosynthesis and coordination of nitrogen utilization with carbon allocation to biomass
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes cellulose accumulation, thicker secondary cell walls, larger leaf area, enhanced nitrogen-use efficiency, greater biomass, and increased grain yield, especially under low nitrogen; the indica 9311 allele is stronger than the japonica Nipponbare allele
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Natural variation at MYB61 underlies the colocalized qNLA1/qCel1 QTL affecting nitrogen-mediated leaf area and cellulose content between indica and japonica rice. 2. CRISPR knockout of MYB61 reduced cellulose content, wall thickness, leaf area, internode diameter, and NUE, while complementation restored these traits. 3. The indica 9311 allele shows stronger transcription than the japonica allele because the japonica promoter contains a helitron insertion, and this stronger allele improves biomass and grain yield under reduced nitrogen.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: QTL mapping and fine mapping; NIL analysis; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; genetic complementation with allele-specific promoter constructs; qPCR; SEM of cell walls; cellulose quantification; field trials under different urea supplies
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in developing internodes and young panicles; induced by low nitrogen
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in developing internodes and panicles; induced in leaf sheaths by low NH4NO3; 9311 allele responds more strongly than Nipponbare allele
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native MYB61 promoters from Nipponbare and 9311 in complementation constructs; promoter activity compared in ProMYB61NP:LUC and ProMYB619311:LUC reporters
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low nitrogen or limited NH4NO3 supply (0.2 mM NH4NO3 in hydroponics; low/zero urea in field) enhances relevance of the phenotype; 9311 allele particularly beneficial under reduced N
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Nitrogen limitation; direct activation by GRF4
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Cellulose biosynthetic program/secondary cell wall formation genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key determinant of cellulosic biomass biogenesis and nitrogen-use divergence between indica and japonica rice
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious harvest index penalty reported; effect on yield advantage diminished at high N (300 kg/ha urea)
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: The indica 9311 allele is an elite variation for improving NUE, biomass, and grain yield in japonica backgrounds, especially under reduced N input; useful for marker-assisted introgression and pyramiding with GRF4
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of developing internodes; rice accession resequencing (~15×), NCBI PRJNA407820; EBI ERP001143; genome assemblies GCA_001433935.1, GCA_002151415.1, GCA_009797565.1, GCA_001889745.1, GCA_003865215.1
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter variation rather than coding-sequence change is the main causal difference in this study; a helitron insertion in japonica promoters compromises transcription and likely GRF4-mediated activation.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Growth-Regulating Factor (GRF) family transcription factor; target of OsmiR396
Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: ZH11; 9311 allele referenced in NIL pyramiding; GS2 gain-of-function line
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major structural biomass polymer supporting plant growth and yield
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Internodes/cell wall of sclerenchyma fiber cells
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Coordination of carbon and nitrogen metabolism; activation of cellulose biogenesis via MYB61
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes MYB61 expression, cellulose accumulation, thicker cell walls, increased biomass, enhanced NUE, and increased grain yield; gain-of-function GS2 increases these traits, while grf4 loss-of-function mutants reduce them
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GRF4 is an upstream positive regulator of MYB61 and is located in the qNLA2 NUE QTL region. 2. Gain- and loss-of-function GRF4 mutants show corresponding increases and decreases in cellulose content, wall thickness, and biomass traits. 3. GRF4 directly binds the MYB61 promoter and activates transcription, and stacking 9311 GRF4 with 9311 MYB61 further improves NUE and grain yield.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; gain-of-function GS2 mutant; qPCR of MYB61 expression; Arabidopsis protoplast transactivation assays; ChIP-PCR; EMSA; NIL pyramiding field tests
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Accumulates under limited nitrogen according to cited and discussed results
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Low-N inducible context; assessed functionally in seedling leaf sheaths and mature internodes
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low nitrogen enhances GRF4 accumulation and makes the GRF4-MYB61 module especially relevant; field urea levels 0, 150, 300 kg/ha
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Low nitrogen availability; release from OsmiR396 repression in GS2 mutant due to disrupted miR396-binding site
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: MYB61; previously reported N metabolism genes including GS1.2 used as positive control context
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly activates MYB61 transcription by binding its promoter
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Integrative regulator linking nitrogen utilization to cellulosic biomass production through MYB61
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No harvest index penalty reported in pyramiding lines; benefits most evident under limited N
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable NUE-improving allele for breeding; stacking the 9311 GRF4 and MYB61 alleles provides additive gains in biomass, grain yield, and NUE
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Rice accession resequencing (~15×), NCBI PRJNA407820; EBI ERP001143
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The confirmed GRF4 binding site in the MYB61 promoter lies adjacent to the japonica helitron insertion, supporting the model that the insertion compromises GRF4 transactivation.
